verdict: 2026-07-01 20:02:57 PDT - Sound shape. The CI addition follows the ratified estate-wide standard: shfmt is installed as a pinned, checksum-verified binary like gitleaks, the check uses `-d -i 2 -ci` matching the reformat, and it runs on the workflow's PR and push-to-main events rather than being added to the minimal local gate. I do not see a high-leverage flag-drift concern: centralizing this into another script/config would add surface area without a concrete win for this repo, and the current command is explicit next to ShellCheck. I also do not see an unratified reformat footgun; the compact one-line reflow is exactly the amended, Thomas-approved adoption behavior. Verified: full changed files read, `shfmt -d -i 2 -ci $(git ls-files '*.sh')` exits clean, and `bash -n` is clean. The local gate could not run in this read-only sandbox because its temp repo creation is blocked.
